domain: coral reef ecology (mass-spawning lunar synchronization via cryptochrome + post-sunset-darkness cues)
specific_mechanism: Broadcast coral spawning is synchronized to a small window after the full moon. The mechanistic cue is NOT moon-tide but moonlight intensity changes detected via cryptochrome photoreceptors and (more recently shown) the post-sunset darkness gap between sunset and moonrise. A single colony's gametes are useless without the colony cloud — synchrony is a fertility-cascade phenomenon: each individual times its release against a shared external clock to maximize gamete encounter probability.
llm_application: Inference-batch broadcast cue: in pipeline-parallel LLM serving, individual requests are processed asynchronously and KV-cache pressure forces eviction at unpredictable times. Instead, gate all decode-emit operations on a shared 'lunar' clock — a per-pool global broadcast pulse driven by an external scheduler — so token emissions across requests align into 'spawn windows', exploiting GPU SIMT-batching efficiency. Cascade effect: alignment of decoder emissions enables population-level memory reuse and reduces per-request KV churn analogously to coral-fertilization efficiency.
novelty_claim: Continuous batching (HF blog), Flow-controlled scheduling (2604.11001), FairBatching (2510.14392), Dynamic Micro-Batch IoT (MDPI), and the December 2025 Optimal Scheduling theory paper all address inference batching. None specifically frames inference scheduling as a lunar-cue mass-spawning event with a coral-cascade-like fertilization-efficiency optimum at a population scale.
